bull.label: Bull
bull.whatHappens: Urban robotaxi unit economics cross internal hurdles by year three; two additional major metros approve expanded driverless domains; personal-auto combined ratios inflect as frequency benefits appear in disclosed data.
bull.whatConfirms: Sequential permit expansion, rising autonomous passenger-mile share without sustained safety regressions, and insurer commentary acknowledging structural frequency improvement.
bull.positionImpact: Long AV compute and stack exposure benefits most; short personal-lines hedges work but require discipline on timing. Legacy OEM longs that lack fleet leverage underperform.

base.label: Base
base.whatHappens: Deployment proceeds in a stair-step pattern with periodic pauses after incidents; insurance and liability adjust slowly; ownership declines gradually in dense cities while suburbs lag.
base.whatConfirms: Regulatory two-steps-forward-one-back pattern, mixed insurer results, and gradual residual pressure concentrated in urban segments.
base.positionImpact: Thesis remains valid but path-dependent; position sizing favors liquid enablers over illiquid single-operator bets. Shorts require tight risk controls around earnings noise.

bear.label: Bear
bear.whatHappens: A persistent safety or political backlash restricts domains; insurers retrench without clear frequency data; OEMs delay programs and consolidate spend on driver-assist only.
bear.whatConfirms: Permit revocations or moratoria, rising severity narratives, and capex cuts to L4 programs with no offsetting regulatory progress.
bear.positionImpact: AV pure-plays and aggressive longs are hurt; personal-auto insurers may mean-revert; cash-rich platforms with diversified revenue outperform narrow autonomy bets.
